cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is about what we know about garage sales based on the provided passage. First, I need to recall the information given in the text. The passage mentions that weekend outdoor garage sales have become very much a part of American culture. It also says they are usually held in spring and fall when the weather is good. The homeowner wants to clear out accumulated items they no longer need, and they advertise in a local paper. The goods are in good condition, and you can find great buys if you have time and patience. They display items on the driveway or front lawn. Examples of items include old magazines, books, paintings, bikes, ice skates, clothes, and appliances. Also, if someone needs to sell their home or is moving, they might give things away for much less than their true worth. Now, looking at the options: A) It is only held in the spring of the year. But the text says "usually in spring and fall," so "only" is incorrect. So A is wrong. B) The goods are totally useless. The passage says the opposite—they are in good condition. So B is wrong. C) Garage sales are popular with Americans. The text states they have become very much part of American culture, which implies popularity. So C is likely correct.
